questions.q: Describe the structure and function of the root system in flowering plants. How does it help in the absorption of water and minerals? Explain the types of root systems found in flowering plants.
questions.answer: The root system in flowering plants consists of primary roots and secondary roots. The primary root grows straight down into the soil, while secondary roots arise from the lateral side of the primary root and grow horizontally into the soil. The root hairs are present on the surface of the root and help in the absorption of water and minerals. The root system also stores food and helps in the support of the plant. There are three main types of root systems found in flowering plants: Tap root system, Fibrous root system, and Adventitious root system. Tap root system is characterized by a single main root that grows deep into the soil, while Fibrous root system consists of a large number of small roots that grow in all directions. Adventitious root system is found in plants that grow in water or floating plants.
marking_scheme: Describe the structure and function of the root system (1 mark)
Explain how it helps in the absorption of water and minerals (1 mark)
Explain the types of root systems found in flowering plants (3 marks)

questions.q: Explain the structure and function of the stem in flowering plants. How does it help in the support and transport of the plant? Describe the types of stems found in flowering plants.
questions.answer: The stem in flowering plants consists of epidermis, cortex, and stele. The epidermis is the outermost layer of the stem and helps in the protection of the stem. The cortex is the middle layer of the stem and consists of parenchyma cells that store food. The stele is the innermost layer of the stem and consists of vascular tissues that help in the transport of water and minerals. The stem also produces lateral shoots and roots. The stem helps in the support and transport of the plant by providing a framework for the leaves and flowers to grow on. There are two main types of stems found in flowering plants: Erect stem and Decumbent stem. Erect stem grows straight up into the air, while Decumbent stem grows along the ground.
marking_scheme: Describe the structure and function of the stem (2 marks)
Explain how it helps in the support and transport of the plant (2 marks)
Describe the types of stems found in flowering plants (1 mark)

questions.q: Describe the structure and function of the leaf in flowering plants. How does it help in the process of photosynthesis? Explain the types of leaves found in flowering plants.
questions.answer: The leaf in flowering plants consists of epidermis, mesophyll, and vascular tissues. The epidermis is the outermost layer of the leaf and helps in the protection of the leaf. The mesophyll is the middle layer of the leaf and consists of palisade and spongy parenchyma cells that perform photosynthesis. The vascular tissues are present in the leaf veins and help in the transport of water and minerals. The leaf helps in the process of photosynthesis by converting light energy into chemical energy. There are two main types of leaves found in flowering plants: Simple leaf and Compound leaf. Simple leaf is a single leaf that grows from a node, while Compound leaf is a leaf that is divided into leaflets.
marking_scheme: Describe the structure and function of the leaf (2 marks)
Explain how it helps in the process of photosynthesis (2 marks)
Explain the types of leaves found in flowering plants (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the structure and function of the flower in flowering plants. How does it help in the reproduction of the plant? Describe the parts of the flower and their functions.
questions.answer: The flower in flowering plants consists of sepals, petals, stamens, and pistils. The sepals are the green, leaf-like structures that protect the flower. The petals are the colorful parts of the flower that attract pollinators. The stamens are the male reproductive organs of the flower and consist of anther and filament. The pistils are the female reproductive organs of the flower and consist of stigma, style, and ovary. The flower helps in the reproduction of the plant by producing seeds. The parts of the flower have specific functions: the sepals protect the flower, the petals attract pollinators, the stamens produce pollen, and the pistils produce ovules.
marking_scheme: Describe the structure and function of the flower (2 marks)
Explain how it helps in the reproduction of the plant (2 marks)
Describe the parts of the flower and their functions (1 mark)

questions.q: Describe the structure and function of the fruit in flowering plants. How does it help in the dispersal of seeds? Explain the types of fruits found in flowering plants.
questions.answer: The fruit in flowering plants consists of pericarp, seed coat, and endosperm. The pericarp is the outer layer of the fruit that develops from the ovary wall. The seed coat is the outer layer of the seed that provides protection. The endosperm is the food storage tissue of the seed. The fruit helps in the dispersal of seeds by providing a protective covering and a means of transportation. There are three main types of fruits found in flowering plants: Drupe, Berry, and Capsule. Drupe is a fruit that has a hard stone in the center, Berry is a fruit that has a fleshy pericarp, and Capsule is a fruit that splits open to release the seeds.
marking_scheme: Describe the structure and function of the fruit (2 marks)
Explain how it helps in the dispersal of seeds (2 marks)
Explain the types of fruits found in flowering plants (1 mark)
